utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , carolyn . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good . you're ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so carolyn is a 49-year-old female here for follow-up of her chronic problems . so , how are you doing , carolyn ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . um , my osteoarthritis has been flaring up a little bit lately , um , so i wan na get- talk about that primarily . so , it's mostly in um , my elbows and it's really been bothering me . i've been sitting at the computer , at the desk more , um , in the office so i do n't know if it's being stagnant in that position all day has um , increased flare-ups for that , but i wanted to talk to you about that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , sure . and , are you right or left-handed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm right-handed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and does one elbow bother you more than the other ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my right elbow does bother me more than the other , but primarily , i'm typing all day versus writing , so it- it flares up in both .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , and um , any other joint pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , just- just really focused on my elbows .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and any numbing or tingling in your hands ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , sometimes . when i'm typing for long periods of time , i feel a little bit of numbing , um , i try to shake out my arms a little bit . um , just to kind of relieve that um , sensation .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , and i know that you've had this also , had this history of gout . and the last episode you had was about three months ago . you had some inflammation of your toe . have you had any other issues with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i have n't . um , the medication you gave me really controlled it , and i have n't seen a flare-up since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , and how are you doing with your psoriasis ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's been under control too . that has n't been a- a major problem for me either .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i- i know that we had given you some clobetasol for your scalp . is that- so that's doing okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that's doing a lot better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . um , all right , well , i'd like to go ahead and do a physical exam on you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's first look at your vital signs . hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . great , so everything looks good from that perspective . um , so looking at you , i do n't appreciate any cervical lymphadenopathy . your heart is a nice regular rate and rhythm , and your lungs sound really clear . on your right elbow , you do have some edema and inflammation of your right olecranon and there's some tenderness and an effusion right there . so um , does that hurt when i press it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that does hurt .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and when i turn your arm , do you have pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that hurts a bit too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so she has pain- to palpation of the olecranon bursa and pain with pronation and supination . and when you flex- and when you bend it and straighten it , does that hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it does and it's a bit stiff too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so pain with flexion and extension of the right arm . uh , your abdomen is nice and soft , and there's no lower extremity edema . uh , let's go ahead and take a look at some of your labs . you know , you have a lot of arthritis for someone so young , so we had sent off that autoimmune panel . hey , dragon . show me the autoimmune labs . hey , dragon . show me the autoimmune profile . hey , dragon . show me the esr . okay , so that's good . hey , dragon . show me the uric acid . okay . all right , well , good . that's great . so , you know , it does n't look like you have any systemic arthritis , or sys- systemic inflammatory in process going on . so , my impression of you at this time is that from a gout standpoint , everything is nice and stable . i think that we should continue you on the colchicine 0.6 milligrams twice a day as needed for flare-ups of your gout . um , and then for your osteoarthritis , i'd like to go ahead and order a right elbow x-ray . hey , dragon . order a right elbow x-ray , and then i'd like to prescribe some meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day for the pain and the swelling .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . order meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day . and then i'd like to um , just see those- how- the x-ray show- shows up and the results of that before we refer you for any type of physical therapy , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then from a psoriasis standpoint , i think everything is stable . we'll continue you on the steroid cream for your scalp , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , take care .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 49-year-old female presenting for follow-up of chronic problems.
Osteoarthritis
The patient reports osteoarthritis flare-ups in her elbows, potentially exacerbated by prolonged computer use.
The doctor asks if the patient is right or left-handed.
The patient is right-handed.
The doctor asks if one elbow bothers the patient more than the other.
The patient reports the right elbow bothers her more, but both flare up due to typing.
The doctor asks if the patient has any other joint pain.
The patient denies other joint pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has numbness or tingling in her hands.
The patient reports occasional numbness and tingling in her hands when typing, relieved by shaking her arms.
Gout
The doctor notes the patient's last gout episode was three months ago in her toe.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any other gout issues.
The patient denies further gout issues and reports the previous medication controlled the flare-up.
Psoriasis
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with her psoriasis.
The patient reports her psoriasis is under control.
The doctor asks if the clobetasol for the patient's scalp is working.
The patient reports the scalp treatment is working better.
Physical exam findings: normal neck, heart, lungs, and abdomen; no lower extremity edema; right olecranon edema, inflammation, tenderness, and effusion.
The doctor asks if the right elbow hurts when pressed.
The patient confirms pain upon palpation of the elbow.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain when her arm is turned.
The patient confirms pain with arm rotation.
The doctor documents pain with pronation, supination, and palpation of the olecranon bursa.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain when bending and straightening her arm.
The patient reports pain and stiffness with flexion and extension.

The doctor confirms pain with flexion and extension, notes normal labs, and plans to continue colchicine 0.6 mg twice daily for gout, order a right elbow x-ray, and prescribe meloxicam 15 mg once daily for osteoarthritis.
Continue colchicine 0.6 mg twice a day as needed for gout.
Prescribe meloxicam 15 mg once a day for pain and swelling.
The doctor will review the x-ray results before considering a physical therapy referral.
The doctor advises continuing the steroid cream for psoriasis.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
